what: M=256 quant kernel BSN=128 NW=4 (double K-width per block): 96 blocks for 256x2880x512, better memory coalescing for bf16 loads, vs current BSN=64 NW=2 with 192 blocks
why: v141 tested BSN=128 but with NUM_ITER=4 (only 24 blocks). BSN=128 NW=4 with NUM_ITER=1 gives ~96-180 blocks depending on K — enough for CU utilization while doubling coalesced load width per block
